content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light and an intentional act of critical thought, existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate these two distinct steps: a passive 'reflection' step that bounces the LLM’s own output off an implicit external standard of correctness to surface inconsistencies, followed by an active 'reflection' step that explicitly revises the model’s stored working memory. This split process outperforms current monolithic self-reflection approaches by leveraging the same dual meaning inherent to the core concept that already unites both domains.
